column1: We have to pay the electricity bill before Friday.

column2: We can pay it if we want to.
We must pay it.
We've already paid it.

column1: You don't need to buy me a birthday present.

column2: You must buy me a birthday present.
It's not necessary to buy me a birthday present.
You mustn't buy me a birthday present.

column1: I have to do some work on my project this evening.

column2: I haven't got time to do the work.
I've already done the work.
I need to do the work.

column1: Lenny didn't have to see the head teacher after all.

column2: It wasn't necessary for Lenny to see the head teacher.
Lenny went to see the head teacher.
Lenny is waiting to see the head teacher.
They can run if they want to.
Students don't like running.
Running isn't allowed.
They have to fasten their seatbelts now.
They don't have to fasten their seatbelts.
They can fasten their seatbelts.

column1: Mr Reed had to go to the police station to answer some questions.

column2: Mr Reed was able to go to the police station.
Mr Reed forgot to go to the police station.
Mr Reed was obliged to go to the police station.

column1: It's kind of you to offer to help, but you really don't need to.

column2: I don't want you to help me.
Your help isn't necessary.
You won't be able to help me.
